title: scilla autumnalis subsp. fallax
intro: Scilla autumnalis subsp. fallax, also known as fall-flowering scilla or autumnal squill, is a subspecies of the scilla autumnalis plant that belongs to the Asparagaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its bulbs, which contain sulfur compounds and other bioactive molecules that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Scilla autumnalis subsp. fallax include its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of allergies. In herbal preparations, the bulbs of Scilla autumnalis subsp. fallax are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and side effects.
